flashcards.back: The model memorizes training data (including noise) and performs well on training but poorly on unseen test data.
The model is too simple to capture underlying patterns, performing poorly on both training and test data.
Regularization (L1/L2), cross-validation, and collecting more training data.

flashcards.front: What is the curse of dimensionality?
flashcards.back: As features increase, data becomes sparse in high-dimensional space, making pattern detection harder and requiring exponentially more data.
When training data is not representative of the real-world distribution, leading to poor generalization.

flashcards.front: Why is interpretability a challenge in ML?
flashcards.back: Complex models (deep nets, ensembles) act as black boxes — accurate but hard to explain, which is critical in healthcare, finance, and law.
